after-image effect We use the after-image effect for a completely new brand experience For the first advertising commercial that doesn't use a directly visible logo But by doing so generates a more intensive connection to the target group We developed a cinema ad for BMW motorcycles that turn spectators into astonished fans It does this by using an after-image of the brand to literally get inside people's heads Here's how it worked While the cinema ad was playing we illuminated the BMW logo with a harmless photo flash onto the audience's eyes The audience at first only perceives a bright flash during the ad However, when they were asked to close their eyes at the end of the ad They were surprised to see the BMW logo as an after-image Here is the original ad Every day people ask me why I race Why I take a turn at 200 Why do I go full power I Always give them the same answer I am living my dream What dream they ask just close your eyes and say and look deep into yourself Maybe So that means if you're not doing a strategy every seven minutes, you're going to lose your distraction. Does that make sense? Are you getting this? OK. And so you have to have a strategy every seven seconds to hook them back into your presentation. He was so good at stage anchoring. He would tell jokes, and there would be a chair right here. He would tell jokes, anchor down the chair, tell jokes, anchor down the chair. Then he'd walk away. The people not knowingly would stop laughing. He would just turn around and start walking over to that chair, and people would start cracking up. So what he was doing was using stage anchoring. They didn't realize it, but the subconscious would start cracking up. Same thing with the sales process. So let me ask this question. So is it OK if I share a story with you? OK, let me share the story with you. So when I first started my company, you've got to picture this. I was a football trainer. All I loved to do was watch play football and sports and all this. I had no idea about becoming a business man. So I started my business on my credit card right after surgery, and it was a very extremely tough time. Hurt my ego because I was this physical guy that was no physicalist. I had to have a cane and a walker and all this stuff. So I'm starting my business, and it's exploding. And we're doing all these events. And we became a promoter. Does anybody know the difference between a promoter and a speaker? Do you guys know what that is? You know that when you do an event, that you're the promoter, and then sometimes you bring outside speakers in, you guys get that. So what will happen is we were a promoter. So we would put all these little real estate foreclosure workshops, or how to make money in real estate, events. And we started doing a lot of events. We got up to about 50 events a month. We became very good at filling rooms. And what would happen is we would have these other speakers, these expert speakers, come up on stage, and they would razzle and dazzle them, and they say, everybody, get up right now and get to that back table. And people would run back there and buy their product or service. So it was a good business for us. So we were doing back then about a million bucks a month. So pictures, I remember sitting in my office, my other two partners were sitting there in this round table. And we're looking at our P&L statement at the end of the month. Like, man, we just took in a million bucks. So our accountant comes in, and he's talking about how much profit we had that month. And we made a million bucks in revenue. Guess what our profit was? Zero. I'm like, wait a minute. We're looking at our profit and loss, but marketing costs, room costs. We're looking at the expense of employees, the commissions, credit card fees. We're looking at all this. And then at the bottom, we see commission to the other speaker, 50% of a million bucks. Half a million dollars. They're like, half a million dollars went out the window to these other speakers. So we're sitting in the office. And my partner's like, we've got to find a solution. These speakers come to our event. They take all the money out. There's a half a million dollars of money right there. We've got to figure out how to get that back. And I'm sitting there. I'm like, yeah. I said, well, I think we should do something. I think we should do our own events. I think one of us will get our own speaker, create our own product, and then we don't have to give 50% away. And my partner's like, yeah, I think that's a good idea. So let's decide right now that we're going to have our own speakers and our own product. And I'm like, I agree. That's a good idea. So my partners look at each other and says, you know what, Dave? We decided that we think you should be the speaker. And I'm like, well, hold on for a minute. You guys don't get this. I just came out of Baxarousia not too long ago. I'm a football trainer. My degree is in sports medicine. I'm not a speaker, guys. And they said, Dave, we think you should be the speaker. I came up with every excuse in the book. You know what? If you know me, what you guys will get to know me, I take any challenge on. I have no fear. I said, you know what? I'll take that challenge. So I'm like, OK, now I've got to learn how to become a speaker. So I was like, man, I need to go like a speaker training course, a boot camp or something. So I find out there's one going on in Vegas. So I fly to Vegas. I go to this four-day speaker training. They teach me how to become a speaker and teach me all this stuff. And so I fly back and I land. I come to my office and the part is like, hey, man, how did that training go? And I'm like, it's a boot camp. I learned some cool stuff. He's like, well, great. Now that you're a speaker, I'm like, no, I'm a speaker. I just went to a four-day boot camp. They said, we just got your first gig. And I'm like, OK. I said, when is it? They go, tomorrow. I go, well, well, well, hold on for a minute, guys. We don't have a presentation. We didn't have a product done yet. What do you mean? I just got done with this. No, you've got to go tomorrow. Oh my gosh. So I get on the plane. I fly up to South Carolina. And there's a real estate investor club in South Carolina. So I fly up there. Our guy used to work for us. His name was David Hans. Picked me up in the car. I had this cheap suit on. Oh my, it was embarrassing. I can't believe I was this bad. So I pull up. We pull up this VFW haul. I'm sitting in this car. Picture this. And it's dark out. It's real dark and gray up there. And I'm sitting here. I'm looking at this VFW haul. I'm thinking, oh my gosh. I got to give a presentation in 30 minutes. We don't even have a product done yet. I don't even have a presentation really. I've never done this before. I've only done really a couple real estate deals. I'm thinking, oh my gosh. And I'm sitting there going this. I'm like, you know what? I hope it snows. I'm hoping a tornado comes. I'm like, please let a hurricane come up here or something. And as I'm thinking this, I looked up. And my market manager, his name is David Hans, looked at me and his eyes were like this. And I'm like, what? He goes, Dave, are you going to be OK? I'm like, Dave, you're pitch white. And I was like, all right, let's go in. So I remember, get this. I shut the door, walk up to this re-group. And I open the door and squeaks. And I walk in. I see all these people. I see this banner that Dustin put up. It says, Dave Van Hoos, foreclosure king. I'm like, oh my gosh. I see another Dave Van Hoos, foreclosure expert. And I see everybody pointing. I'm like, oh my gosh. It's hitting me even more. I'm like, I need to find the bathroom. Where's the bathroom at? He goes over here. So I'm in the bathroom, doing my thing. God comes in. He goes, Dave, are you ready? I said, yeah, hold on. OK, I'm ready. And he introduced me. And I got up on stage. And wham! Whole world stopped. I was in this zone, man. I was doing my presentation, doing this thing, talking about the product that we didn't really have done yet. And I said, everybody get up right now and get to that back table right now. And man, they all went running back there. And I made $50,000 the first time out. Whooo! I was excited, man. I was like, oh my gosh. And so I'm flying on the plane. I'm like, I'm the hero of my company. It's been $50,000 the first time out. And I'm flying back. And I love listening to personal development, by the way, and reading. So I'm listening to Tony Robbins. As I'm flying back, listening to Tony Robbins, Tony Robbins, like, this is Tony Robbins. I've got to tell you, I wasn't the best speaker in the world. And so I spoke four times a day, seven days a week. I'm like, I want to be like Tony Robbins. So I come back and tell my person, hey, listen, I want to speak twice a day, seven days a week. So I go, OK. So they started booking me. Well, we found out after the fifth day, I lost my voice. So I was spraying, charcoal, I mean, grogging all salt and all this stuff. But that's what happened, is I wanted to become the best. And that's just my sports background. So I really want to challenge each and every one of you to do the best at what you do. Be the world best in whatever business you're in. Just be the best. Play full out. And so I'm doing these seminars. And it was so much fun, because I got to test all these little things that I'm sharing with you right now. And learning this process. And I'm telling you, it was so much fun. And what I want to do is I want to share with you this process. And I'm going to tell you, anybody can do this. Because if I can do it, you can do it. Absolutely. And I'm telling you, I was a football trainer of all people. So we want to start with humor. I just want to tell you a quick little story about how I became a speaker. And I got to tell you, after I learned my presentation, it was so much fun. I also want to share this little trick that I learned. I hated selling. I was so embarrassed to sell stuff back then. I hated it. I didn't want to sell. I was literally, I love it now. But what happened to me is that I made this shift. And the shift to me was, I did this one event. And it was in Tampa, Florida, right here. There was about 100 people at this event. And I'm doing my pitch. And I said, everybody get up right now and get to that back table and buy the foreclosure system. I always knew I was going to run it back there. And I'll never forget this guy. Little short guy, his name was Jose. Jose comes back and goes, Dave, Dave, you're broke. Me, me, me want foreclosure system. Me want to become real estate millionaire. I'm like, OK, go ahead and sign up here. He goes, me just move to this country. And me give you my last $1,000. And I'm like, well, maybe you shouldn't be here. Maybe this isn't for you. Because I knew the odds of him not having any money, not seeking the language. I'm like, maybe you shouldn't get this system. No, no, no. He's like, me want foreclosure system. Me want to become real estate millionaire. Me move to America. And he signed up. And I went home that day. And I felt so bad. I'm thinking, oh my god, what did I do? I took this poor guy's last $1,000, just moved to this country. I couldn't sleep that night. I felt so bad. And my father used to work for my company because he was too tired and nothing to do. And I always had to say, who made it and who didn't? This is in real estate. And because some guys made a whole bunch of money in real estate, and then a lot of people didn't do nothing. Went home, put books on their shelves, and they do nothing. So I kept thinking, what was this cause? And so I found out from my dad, four months later, that this guy took the system and made a half a million dollars investing in real estate. And he told me that. I'm reading this book by Brian Tracy. And it says, who are you to be God, to determine who can and can't win in this world? All's your job is to give the best information, the best system to help people. And that hit me. Who was I to determine who can get this product and who couldn't? And I saw it time and time again, because I've been doing this for a long time. People that you would think a long shot. Somebody, this guy just had all these things. They'd have the education, the time, the money, and they made it. And so what happened to me is a shift. I found out and realized, and you guys got to get this one thing. You can't change anybody's life in 90 minutes. You cannot change anybody's life in nine minutes. They can come to your event. You can teach them all the stuff you want to teach them in 90 minutes. They're going to go back to their job that they're having troubles with, back to their bad relationships, back to being overweight, back to all that things. You can change their life if they buy your product, get into your coaching program, and you can help them. So I want you to make that shift. You're not here to sell people. You're here to help them, to solve their problems and help them. I believe in every ounce of my soul that if I didn't get everybody to sign up for my system, that they were going to go out and hurt themselves and hurt people too. Does that make sense? So I want you to make that shift. So when you're up there, don't think you're selling people. You're helping people. Does that make sense? So let's get back to this. So we talked about we want to basically frame them in our presentations. We want to tell them what we're going to tell them. Make them a promise like I talked about. We also want to make sure that we tell them about the opportunity. Don't let them wait until the end of your presentation to go over the opportunity. Let them know. Let it be seen. Let them know. People respect you so much better when you're very honest with them. Don't hide out your product and say, oh, by the way, it's here. No. Remember I said, once they experience something fully, it vanishes. So let them know that you have something to offer them. So if you're doing the one-on-one sales or even on your webinar, let them know. Hey, great guys, at the end of my webinar today, I've got something amazing I'm going to share with you. Let them know. Some people are like, oh, he's trying to sell him and get mad. Well, guess what? He wasn't your customer anyway. Does that make sense? So one of the things I tell a lot of people is a lot of speakers and a lot of businesses are leaving money on the table. You guys are leaving money on a table by not having a sales process that works. So one of the things I want to tell you a quick little story about how I really turned it up. I had a big challenge. How many of you guys know this guy here? I'm going to see him on TV. Dan Merle. He's a guy. He's in real estate. And he's been doing a lot of presentations. He's a big info marketer in the real estate. He teaches you how to buy and sell houses. And so I fly out to San Diego to sit down and help him. And I'm helping with this presentation. I'm showing him how to close better. And I'm doing all this stuff. And then Dan goes to me and goes, you know, Dave, and what happens to a lot of these speakers is they like to go and speak on stages and travel and have fun. And a lot of events are in Orlando. And instead of me flying all the way out there, why don't you just do my presentation for me? Just go up there, sell the system, and I'll pay you. And that way I don't have to fly all the way across here. Because it takes me a day to fly and a day back, lose two days. I'll make sure. I love this. No problem. I can just drive over here and do it for you. No big deal. So I'm helping with his presentation, teaching him what to do. And he's getting married. So I guess there's a big thing. I guess if you're a speaker in your mail and you're just getting married, something, a shift happens in your business. And he calls me. So he goes, Dave, I want you to do these presentations for me. I'm like, hey, I have no problem. Don't worry about it. I can do this in my sleep. So I get to the airport. And I fly home. And I land in Tampa. And then Dan calls me. He goes, hey, Dave. I'm like, hey, Dan. He goes, hey, you know how I said that you could do my presentations? And I'll pay you for it. And I'm like, yeah. He goes, well, I got your first gig. I'm like, OK, very cool, man. Like, where's it at? He goes, Orlando. I'm like, all right, no big deal. I'll drive over there in the morning, speak for an hour. And then I'll jump on my boat after. No big deal. I'm like, when is the seminar? He's like, tomorrow. I'm like, tomorrow. I said, dude, I just helped you. I don't even know your product. I really don't know your presentation. I mean, and I stopped. And I said, you know what? No problem. I'll be there. So I hung up the phone. And now I'm thinking to my mind, oh my god, I don't know this guy's product. I don't even know what's in it. I'm thinking all this stuff. I don't even know his social proof stories. I mean, I went through his presentation once. And I'm thinking to myself, all this stuff. And not only that, I've never even wholesaled a property. So I drive over. Get this. So I drive over Saturday morning. And I'm just kind of looking at his presentation again. And I pull up. And I get to this hotel. And there's about 400 people at this seminar. I'm thinking, oh my god, dude, there's 400 people there. You're going to make a fool of yourself. I don't even know this guy's presentation. So I get up there. And there's a promoter there. His name was Colin. And Colin goes, hey, Dave. Hey, he goes, hey, where's Stan at? I'm like, what do you mean? He goes, yeah, Stan's up at 9 o'clock. I'm like, didn't he tell you? He's like, tell me what? I'm like, he's not coming. He goes, what do you mean he's not coming? I go, he sent me instead. And so the promoter blew up, of course, because he knows he could be in trouble here. And he goes, you know what, Dave, since it's you, I know how you can close. And then he's calling up there and goes, Dan, you never told me this. So I'm like, great. Now the promoter's mad at me. I'm just here. Speak for Than. So then I get up. I go, hey, where's the product at? You know? I'm supposed to show from stage, all the stuff. Than forgot to send the product. So I'm thinking to myself, there's 400 people there. They don't have a product. I don't know the presentation. I really haven't done any wholesale deals. And I got up there, and guess what? It didn't matter. I also, every speaker that was there. Does that make sense? So if I got up here and said, you know, you guys are gonna wanna work with the speaking empire because speaking empire we're gonna help you write your presentation. And then they will come up with the answer. Okay, does that make sense? One of the things that you guys gotta understand is you gotta never slam your competition. You know if you're ever speaking on other people's stages, you're doing webinars, and somebody might ask you, hey what do you think of that guy's product over there? Never ever slam competition. She's like, all right, so I jump on the phone, and I'm like, hey, Zach, how you doing? He goes, you know, Dave, I gave you $10,000 for that presentation. You wrote my coaching pitch. You know, I got 140 people here. You know, I just did it, and I closed 34 people at $25,000. And I'm like, I'm jumping for joy, and I say, what's going on? I go, Zach, just closed 32 people at $25,000. And so what I wanted to say is that you really, if you don't have a high-end package, you wanna make sure you have a high-end package into your product, because people are looking for more hand-held. Let me just share this little shift that has to happen. When I used to do this business, well, I still do the business, when I used to do more of this business five years ago, we used to sell a lot of books and tapes. People back then would run to the back of the room to get the map. We'll call it, you know, the directions of how to become wealthy or how to lose weight. They had run back. But the way the world is today, people don't really want the directions. They don't want the map, the treasure map. They want more done-for-you service. They want more hand-held. They want to pull up in front of your house. You get out, you get in the car with them, you drive up the mountain, round the trees, you stop, you take them to the woods, you find the treasure chest, you take their hand and put it in the treasure, and grab it. That's what they're looking for today. So they are looking. So remember, people are wanting something, you're gonna give it to them, right? People are looking for high-end consulting packages, done-for-you services, more hand-held opportunities. So see if you can add this component into your business. Does that make sense? Can we all do that? Absolutely. So he made $600,000 with the first time out using my coaching pitch. Just imagine, how many of you would like an extra $600,000? Absolutely. This business is an amazing business. I'm telling you, the speaking business, the infomarketer business is an amazing business. Imagine this. I was making $2 million a month. We're making so much money, my partners couldn't count it fast enough. But that's this industry, and I'm telling you right now, what is happening in this economy here, is we're seeing a big shift, entrepreneurship's on the rise. The wave is getting ready to begin. There's a reason why they say smile and dial. Because what happens when you smile, okay, it raises your vocal cords and it goes, your voice goes up two octanes. And when your voice is up two octanes, it bypasses the critical mind and goes into the subconscious mind. So when you smile, there's a strategy behind it. Your voice goes up two octanes and it goes right into the subconscious. So when you get to the close, you're gonna be doing what? Smiling, and you're gonna talk fast, right? The law of reciprocity. How many of you have heard, you must give to receive, right? So there is a law of exchange in this universe, okay? And so one of the things that I've figured out when I first started speaking, when I first started my business, how many of you listened to the secret back when the speaker was hot? And every time that I'd get up on stage and I would go to myself, you know, how many who's seeing the secret, who hasn't seen the secret? And I would give it to one person. And that way, when I was speaking all across the country, because it kind of impacted me, I figured I could impact, a lot of these people are in really bad places. And so I gave it out. And you know what I started noticing? Every time that I gave somebody this secret, guess where they were at the end of my presentation? The back table. So I'm thinking, I'm giving these secrets. I'm like, man, I should give everybody a secret. And so I found out the law of reciprocity. So when you're speaking from stage, you wanna make sure you give stuff out. Okay? You wanna give stuff out.
